sops.title: POS terminal offline or frozen — restore trading
sops.rca: Most POS lockups trace to the local transaction queue filling up when the store link degrades, or a memory leak in the tender module after long uptime. Terminals that have not been rebooted in 14+ days are 4x more likely to freeze during peak trade.
fix: Confirm scope: single till vs whole store (whole store → check network SOP first).
Soft-restart the POS application from the lane controller; do not power-cycle mid-tender.
If frozen mid-payment, verify the last EFT auth on the acquirer portal before re-ringing to avoid double charge.
Clear the offline transaction queue once the link is confirmed healthy.
If hardware fault suspected, swap in the store spare terminal and log an asset ticket.
mitigation: Enable the weekly automated rolling reboot for all lanes (out of trading hours).
Set queue-depth alerting at 200 offline transactions per terminal.
Keep one configured spare terminal per 10 lanes at each store.

sops.title: POS end-of-day batch stuck — trading day not closing
sops.rca: EOD hangs are almost always one unsettled tender (usually a reversed EFT left in PENDING) or a lane that was powered off before cash-up, leaving an open session that blocks the store close.
fix: Identify the blocking lane/session from the EOD console.
Settle or void the pending tender after confirming with the acquirer report.
Force-close the orphaned session, then re-run the EOD job.
Verify the trading-day totals posted to finance before releasing the store.
mitigation: Add a pre-EOD sweep job at 21:30 that flags pending tenders to the store manager.
Train cash-up staff to close lanes via the controller, never by powering off.

sops.title: EFT declines or timeouts spiking
sops.rca: Response code 91 spikes indicate the issuer/switch is unreachable — typically bank-side congestion, an expired connection pool on the EFT switch, or latency on the store link pushing auths past the 8-second timeout.
fix: Check the switch dashboard: is the spike national (bank-side) or one region/store (link-side)?
If bank-side, enable stand-in/offline floor limits per the risk matrix and notify the acquirer.
If link-side, fail the store over to the secondary link and recycle the EFT connection pool.
Communicate tender guidance to affected stores (encourage alternate tender types).
Reconcile stand-in transactions within 24h of restoration.
mitigation: Set latency alerting at 5s p95 on auth round-trips (before customers feel 8s timeouts).
Quarterly failover test of the secondary acquirer route.
Automate connection-pool recycling on sustained error-rate breach.

sops.title: Settlement / dual-message mismatch
sops.rca: Mismatches occur when auth and clearing legs land on different trading dates — usually after an EOD delay, a switch restart mid-batch, or duplicate clearing files from the acquirer.
fix: Pull the recon exception report for the trading date and classify: missing clearing vs duplicate vs amount variance.
For duplicates, confirm with the acquirer before any customer refund.
Re-request the clearing file for missing legs; do not manually journal until file recovery fails.
Post approved adjustments through the recon tool so the audit trail stays intact.
mitigation: Automate day+1 three-way recon (POS vs switch vs acquirer) with exception alerting.
Freeze switch restarts during the settlement window (22:00–02:00).

sops.title: Online checkout failing or timing out
sops.rca: Checkout 5xx bursts usually come from payment-provider latency exhausting the checkout service thread pool. Duplicate orders happen when the client retries while the first payment is still settling — a missing idempotency key on the order-create call.
fix: Check payment-provider status and checkout service saturation (thread pool, p95 latency).
Enable the degraded-checkout mode: queue orders, defer non-critical calls (recommendations, stock badges).
For duplicates, run the dedupe script on orders sharing basket-hash within 10 minutes; cancel and auto-refund the later ones.
For 3-DS loops, verify the ACS return URL matches the current release; roll back the routing change if it drifted.
mitigation: Enforce idempotency keys on order creation.
Circuit-breaker on payment provider with automatic queue-and-retry.
Synthetic checkout probe every 2 minutes with alerting at 2 consecutive failures.

sops.title: Basket / promo total mismatch at checkout
sops.rca: Totals drift when the promo engine and checkout price differently — typically a promotion cached in the basket after it expired, or stacking rules evaluated in different order between web and checkout service.
fix: Reproduce with the exact basket; capture both price traces (web vs checkout).
Flush the promo cache for the affected promotion IDs.
Disable the offending promotion online if exposure is ongoing; honour advertised price at support level.
Log a defect with both traces attached for the stacking-order fix.
mitigation: Contract test between promo engine and checkout on every promo-rule release.
Cache TTL on basket promos capped at 15 minutes.

sops.title: Stock-on-hand not syncing / stale online availability
sops.rca: The nightly SOH sync overruns when the delta feed degrades to a full extract (usually after a failed prior run) — downstream, online availability then serves stale positions all morning.
fix: Check whether last night's job ran delta or full; find the failure that triggered the full extract.
Prioritise the online-fulfilment ranges: push a targeted delta for active online SKUs first.
Temporarily raise safety-stock thresholds online to reduce oversell risk while stale.
Backfill the remainder and verify record counts against the ledger.
mitigation: Split the sync into priority lanes (online-active SKUs first).
Alert if the job passes 60% of its window with under 80% records processed.

sops.title: Negative SOH / receiving not reflecting
sops.rca: Negative positions appear when sales decrement before the matching GRN posts — a sequencing issue between the receiving interface and the sales feed, often after a store scanned goods to the floor before completing receiving.
fix: Identify affected SKUs/stores from the negative-SOH report.
Post the outstanding GRNs; where paperwork is missing, raise a stock adjustment with store sign-off.
Re-run the ledger rebuild for the affected store-days.
Confirm online availability recalculated after correction.
mitigation: Block floor movement scans until receiving completes for the delivery.
Daily negative-SOH exception report to store ops before 08:00.

sops.title: Promo price not applying at till / price file failures
sops.rca: Till/shelf mismatches are nearly always a price file that failed to deploy to a subset of stores overnight — commonly a locked file on the store controller or a store that was offline in the deployment window.
fix: Pull the deployment report: list stores that missed the file.
Re-push the price file to failed stores; verify checksum on the controller.
Until live, authorise manual price override at till per the advertised-price policy.
For double-discounting, suspend the promotion and quantify exposure from transaction logs.
mitigation: Retry-with-alerting on price deployment (3 attempts, then page Merch Systems).
Morning price-verification scan of 20 promo lines per store via the RF gun app.

sops.title: Click-and-collect / orders stuck in allocation
sops.rca: Orders wedge in ALLOCATED when the OMS reserves stock at a node whose picking app never acknowledges — usually the store device is offline or the wave-release scheduler died after a deploy.
fix: Check the OMS→node acknowledgement queue for the affected store/DC.
Restart the wave-release scheduler; confirm heartbeats from picking devices.
For orders past promise time, re-source to the next-best node in bulk via the re-allocation tool.
Trigger customer comms for any revised collection/delivery times.
mitigation: Heartbeat alerting on picking devices per node (silence > 15 min pages Fulfilment Tech).
Auto re-source rule when a node holds an order unacknowledged > 30 min.

sops.title: WRewards points/vouchers not awarding correctly
sops.rca: Missed awards are usually the loyalty API timing out at the till (basket still completes, award event lost). Duplicate vouchers come from the issuing job re-running after a partial failure without an issued-flag check.
fix: For missed points: replay lost award events from the transaction log (T-log) for the affected window.
For till timeouts: raise the till-side timeout to async mode so tender never blocks on loyalty.
For duplicate vouchers: invalidate the later duplicates and notify affected members with a goodwill message.
Verify tier recalculation after any bulk replay.
mitigation: Make voucher issuing idempotent on member+campaign key.
Loyalty calls at till fire-and-forget with guaranteed event queue.

sops.title: Vendor EDI failures (ASN / PO ack / invoices)
sops.rca: Bulk EDI failures cluster around three causes: a vendor system change breaking the message schema, AS2 certificate expiry killing the channel, or a gateway mapping deploy that regressed one document type.
fix: Classify the failures by vendor and document type from the gateway console.
Schema errors: send the vendor the validation report; reprocess once corrected.
Certificate expiry: install the renewed cert and replay the queued messages in sequence.
If GRNs are blocked at a DC, authorise manual receiving against the PO with recon flag.
mitigation: Certificate expiry alerting at 30/7/1 days across all vendor channels.
Canary validation on gateway mapping deploys using golden message samples.

sops.title: Store link down or degraded (4G failover)
sops.rca: Primary-link outages are mostly carrier-side (fibre cuts, exchange faults). Degraded trading on failover happens when non-essential traffic (CCTV backup, patching) is not throttled and saturates the 4G path.
fix: Confirm failover engaged; log the carrier fault with reference number.
Apply the failover QoS profile: POS/EFT priority, block CCTV upload and patch traffic.
If both paths are down, switch the store to offline trading mode per the POS offline SOP.
Monitor until primary restores; verify queue drain and auto-failback.
mitigation: Enforce QoS templates on all store routers, audited monthly for config drift.
Dual-carrier SIMs for the top 100 revenue stores.

sops.title: Mobile app crashes, login failures or content not loading
sops.rca: Post-release crash spikes are typically an untested OS/device combination or a backend contract change the app didn't handle. CDN 403s point at token/key rotation not propagated to the image origin config.
fix: Segment the crash spike by app version, OS and endpoint in the crash tool.
If tied to the new release, halt the phased rollout and feature-flag off the offending screen.
For login failures, check the identity provider status and token clock-skew before app-side fixes.
For CDN 403s, re-sync the origin access keys and purge affected paths.
mitigation: Phased rollouts capped at 10% for 24h with auto-halt on crash-rate breach.
Contract tests between app BFF and backend on every backend release.

sops.title: Gift card / voucher redemption failures
sops.rca: Redemption declines usually mean the gift-card processor session pool expired after a network blip. Multi-redeemable vouchers indicate the redemption flag update failed after approval — a two-phase update without compensation.
fix: Recycle the processor session pool; test with a known-good card.
For a multi-redeemable voucher, block the voucher code immediately and quantify redemptions from logs.
For failed bulk corporate loads, reverse partial loads before re-running to avoid double credit.
Barcode issues at till: verify symbology config on scanner profile against the voucher spec.
mitigation: Make redemption a single atomic operation at the processor.
Daily exception report: vouchers with redemption count > 1.

sops.title: Product master data gaps blocking listing or receiving
sops.rca: Listing blocks after a season load usually mean the supplier onboarding template changed and mandatory attributes arrived blank. Duplicate barcodes and UoM mismatches come from manual captures bypassing PIM validation.
fix: Export the blocked SKU list with missing-attribute reasons from PIM.
Bulk-enrich from the supplier portal where data exists; chase suppliers for the remainder with a deadline.
For duplicate barcodes, deactivate the newer capture and re-issue internal codes where needed.
Replay the PIM→web publish for corrected SKUs and verify image propagation.
mitigation: Validation gate: no SKU leaves PIM without the mandatory attribute set per department.
Lock manual barcode entry; all captures via the supplier portal with checksum validation.
